svg.style: background: transparent; background-color: transparent; color-scheme: light dark;

div.div:
Examples of analytics to monetize:
Top source/destinations (Where do people fly/hotel/car to the most?)
Seasonality (which time of year is the hottest?)
Problems (Top canceled destinations/agancies/)
Costs (? Avg $$$ spent per trip)
...
Etc

Examples of data to report:
Number of trips
Longest trip
Shortest trip
Most travelled month
Cost (?)
...
etc.

border-color: var(--border-color);

div.div: Analytical exports is the main revenue generation.

div.style: border-color: var(--border-color);

ul.li: Scalability -> user growth -> more processing, more metrics
Elasticity -> vary processing based on active bookings today
Data integrity -> data is delivered to the destination (user/3rd party)
Extensibility -> easily add a new metric for exporting.
Performance (? depending on exporter type?) -> metrics are exported sufficiently fast

path.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.div:
Do we need a separate storage? To support some technical requirements? Scalability/performance?
Is it only a logical separation and both can reside in the same database, but different tables?

path.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.div: Reporter and exporter are logically doing the same very similar, but support different business functions, so perhaps can be separated for better adherence to technical requirements?

path.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.div: The type of exporting can be different. Dashboards, subscription to topics/api. Needs to be extensible, elastic (?)
